seoTitle: How to Use Schedule by Zapier (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Trigger recurring Zaps on a daily, weekly, or monthly schedule using Schedule by Zapier, without needing an external app event.
intro: Some automations don't need an external event to kick them off — they just need to run on a timer. This guide covers Schedule by Zapier, a built-in trigger that acts like a cron job, letting you run Zaps daily, weekly, or monthly at a specific time and time zone for recurring reports, reminders, or cleanups.

steps.heading: Add Schedule by Zapier as your trigger
steps.body: Locate the Schedule by Zapier built-in tool and set it as your Zap's trigger step.

steps.heading: Choose your frequency
steps.body: Select how often the Zap should run: hourly, daily, weekly, or monthly.

steps.heading: Set your time zone and execution time
steps.body: Configure the specific local time zone and hour you want the automation to fire.

steps.heading: Manage weekend execution
steps.body: Decide whether the Zap should run on Saturdays and Sundays or skip weekends entirely.

steps.heading: Test with sample payloads
steps.body: Fetch fresh time-based sample data to safely map date and time tokens into your action steps.

steps.heading: Add your action step
steps.body: Connect an app like Slack, Email, or Google Sheets to receive the scheduled event, and consider pairing it with a search action for batch processing.

faq.question: Can I run a Zap every week at a specific time?
faq.answer: Yes, Schedule by Zapier lets you choose weekly frequency along with a specific time zone and execution hour.

faq.question: Can I prevent my Zap from running on weekends?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure weekday and weekend execution preferences to skip Saturdays and Sundays if needed.

faq.question: Does Schedule by Zapier require an external trigger app?
faq.answer: No, it acts as a native, built-in trigger that runs on a timer without needing an external app event.

faq.question: Can I use this to process a batch of records at once?
faq.answer: Yes, pairing Schedule with search actions is a recommended way to process batch data efficiently.
